query_result.output_text: Now I have comprehensive data across all three segments. Let me compile the analysis.
query_result.reasoning:
Now I have comprehensive data to analyze the forecastability of each segment. Let me synthesize my analysis.

**Connectivity Segment - Most Forecastable:**

Revenue Drivers:
- Subscription-based model with ~10.3M subscribers, growing at ~105% YoY
- ARPU disclosed at $66/month (Q1 2026), declining but predictable trajectory
- Revenue recognized ratably over subscription terms
- 164 countries served, diversified geographically
- Enterprise/government contracts provide additional stability
- Mobile connectivity adding 7.4M monthly unique devices

KPIs:
- Subscriber count disclosed quarterly (10.3M as of Q1 2026)
- ARPU disclosed quarterly ($66/month)
- Satellite constellation size (~9,600)
- Network performance metrics (25ms latency, 225 Mbps)
- Mobile unique devices (7.4M)

Cost Structure:
- Well-defined: satellite depreciation (5-year useful life for broadband, 3-year for mobile), terminal/kit costs, ground operating expenses, customer support
- Depreciation sensitivity disclosed: 1-year change = ~$480M impact
- Cost of revenue components clearly enumerated
- SG&A includes marketing and international expansion

Capital Intensity:
- Capex: $4,178M (FY2025), $1,332M (Q1 2026)
- Predictable satellite replacement cycle (5-year life)
- Satellite PP&E: $12,893M gross
- Internal launches from Space segment capitalized

Profitability:
- Consistently profitable: $4,423M operating income (FY2025), $1,188M (Q1 2026)
- Adjusted EBITDA: $7,168M (FY2025), $2,087M (Q1 2026)
- Clear path to continued profitability

Backlog/Visibility:
- Deferred revenue primarily relates to Space and Connectivity enterprise/government contracts
- Subscription model provides recurring revenue visibility

**Space Segment - Moderately Forecastable:**

Revenue Drivers:
- Two revenue streams: Launch Services (point-in-time) and Launch & Development (over time, government contracts)
- Revenue mix: ~63% Launch Services, ~37% Launch & Development (FY2025)
- Customer launches: 43 in FY2025, 7 in Q1 2026
- Government contracts (NSSL, NASA) provide some visibility
- Starship transition creates uncertainty

KPIs:
- Launch cadence disclosed (165 in FY2025, 40 in Q1 2026)
- Customer vs. internal launch split disclosed
- 650 total orbital launches historically
- Starship flight tests (11 completed)

Cost Structure:
- R&D heavy: $3,004M (FY2025), driven by Starship development
- Cost of revenue declining due to reusability
- Flight vehicle depreciation based on expected flights (5-25 flights)
- Impairment charges for damaged vehicles

Capital Intensity:
- Capex: $3,832M (FY2025), $1,052M (Q1 2026)
- Starship infrastructure investment (catch towers, drone ships)
- Terafab manufacturing facility

Profitability:
- Operating loss: $(657)M (FY2025), $(662)M (Q1 2026)
- Adjusted EBITDA: $653M (FY2025), $(351)M (Q1 2026)
- Starship R&D spending creating losses

Uncertainties:
- Starship development timeline uncertain
- Transition from Falcon to Starship creates revenue uncertainty
- Customer launch count declining (43 in FY2025 vs. 45 in FY2024)
- Internal launches don't generate revenue
- Future cost of revenue expected to increase as % of revenue

**AI Segment - Least Forecastable:**

Revenue Drivers:
- Multiple revenue streams: advertising, subscriptions, AI solutions, data licensing
- Advertising revenue subject to seasonality and platform sentiment
- Subscription revenue (6.3M paid subscribers) growing but small
- AI solutions and infrastructure revenue nascent
- Revenue recognition varies: advertising (point-in-time), subscriptions (ratable), data licensing (mixed)

KPIs:
- Combined MAUs: ~550M (Grok + X)
- Grok AI feature MAUs: ~117M
- Grok paid subscribers: ~1.9M
- X Premium subscribers: ~4.4M
- Daily posts: ~350M
- Image/video generation volumes
- BUT: No ARPU disclosed for AI segment, no clear revenue-per-user metrics

Cost Structure:
- R&D: $5,064M (FY2025), $2,379M (Q1 2026) - massive and growing
- GPU depreciation (5-6 year life) driving costs
- Data center infrastructure (20-25 year life)
- Cloud computing costs
- Restructuring charges ($487M in FY2025)
- $3,775M impairment in 2023

Capital Intensity:
- Capex: $12,727M (FY2025), $7,723M (Q1 2026) - massive and accelerating
- GPU hardware: $23,850M gross PP&E
- Data centers: $2,965M gross PP&E + $14,045M in CIP
- Total AI capex dwarfing other segments
- Explicitly described as "capital intensive"

Profitability:
- Operating loss: $(6,355)M (FY2025), $(2,469)M (Q1 2026)
- Adjusted EBITDA: $(1,237)M (FY2025), $(609)M (Q1 2026)
- No clear path to profitability disclosed
- Management explicitly prioritizes investment over profitability

Uncertainties:
- Regulatory investigations (FTC, Irish DPC, CSAM litigation)
- AI model competition and user fluctuation
- "Largely unproven" commercial value
- Orbital AI compute satellites not until 2028+
- Advertising revenue dependent on platform sentiment
- Grok usage "fluctuates significantly" with competitor releases
- App store removal risk
- Data poisoning allegations
- "Spicy" and "Unhinged" modes creating reputational risk

Now let me compile my analysis into a comprehensive answer.
